prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='CWT_rf', seasonality_champion='rf', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: The validation folds reveal extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.88) and high seasonality variance (0.94), indicating models capture patterns very differently. RF-based models dominate both trend tracking (CWT_rf, FT_rf) and seasonality capture (rf, DWT_catboost). Since early horizon specialists (FT_rf, ONLY_DWT_rf) differ from late horizon specialists (DWT_rf, CWT_svr), per-horizon selection could help, but the extreme disagreement strongly favors DBA combination to leverage diverse model behaviors. The true series consistently trends upward across all folds, and RF models best match this pattern.

Since early horizon specialists (FT_rf, ONLY_DWT_rf) differ from late horizon specialists (DWT_rf, CWT_svr), per-horizon selection could help, but the extreme disagreement strongly favors DBA combination to leverage diverse model behaviors. raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k5", "inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35"],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=11 indicates strong per-horizon model variation, favoring best_per_horizon_by_validation and topk_mean_per_horizon_k5. High rmse_spread_ratio=1.88 and high_model_disagreement=true strongly support dba_combination as recommended by pattern_analyst_insights. inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35 adds weighted diversity with shrinkage to handle disagreement (relative_spread_mean=0.071). Only 3 windows requires conservative top_k=5 and shrinkage=0.35 to avoid overfitting."
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: The validation folds reveal extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.88) and high seasonality variance (0.94), indicating models capture patterns very differently. RF-based models dominate both trend tracking (CWT_rf, FT_rf) and seasonality capture (rf, DWT_catboost). Since early horizon specialists (FT_rf, ONLY_DWT_rf) differ from late horizon specialists (DWT_rf, CWT_svr), per-horizon selection could help, but the extreme disagreement strongly favors DBA combination to leverage diverse model behaviors. The true series consistently trends upward across all folds, and RF models best match this pattern.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=11 indicates strong per-horizon model variation, favoring best_per_horizon_by_validation and topk_mean_per_horizon_k5. High rmse_spread_ratio=1.88 and high_model_disagreement=true strongly support dba_combination as recommended by pattern_analyst_insights. inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35 adds weighted diversity with shrinkage to handle disagreement (relative_spread_mean=0.071). Only 3 windows requires conservative top_k=5 and shrinkage=0.35 to avoid overfitting.
